action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17's test_final.py successfully rendered all 11 target views for a validation object using Convention A (exact match to TripoSR's get_spherical_cameras) with per-view flattened rays passed directly to model.renderer(). The output was correctly reshaped to [256,256,3] images. Analysis: The previous model.py used the wrong camera convention and a fragile monkey-patch. I must rewrite model.py with the proven Convention A ray generation and robust renderer output handling. Decision: Write a clean model.py that computes rays per-view, flattens them, calls the internal renderer directly, reshapes the output, composites on white background, and returns RGBA uint8 arrays. Then run a quick end-to-end smoke test of predict.py on one validation object before submitting eval in the next iteration.
action.command: OBJ=$(ls /data/val | head -1) && mkdir -p /tmp/smoke_val && cp -r "/data/val/$OBJ" /tmp/smoke_val/ && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/smoke_val --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output /tmp/smoke_pred.json && test $(ls "/tmp/smoke_pred_renders/$OBJ" | wc -l) -eq 11 && echo "SMOKE_OK"
